root_text.an3.70:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati pubbārāme migāramātupāsāde.
root_text.an3.70:1.3: Atha kho visākhā migāramātā tadahuposathe yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinnaṁ kho visākhaṁ migāramātaraṁ bhagavā etadavoca:
root_text.an3.70:1.4: “handa kuto nu tvaṁ, visākhe, āgacchasi divā divassā”ti?
root_text.an3.70:2.5: Seyyathāpi, visākhe, gopālako sāyanhasamaye sāmikānaṁ gāvo niyyātetvā iti paṭisañcikkhati:
root_text.an3.70:2.6: ‘ajja kho gāvo amukasmiñca amukasmiñca padese cariṁsu, amukasmiñca amukasmiñca padese pānīyāni piviṁsu;
root_text.an3.70:2.7: sve dāni gāvo amukasmiñca amukasmiñca padese carissanti, amukasmiñca amukasmiñca padese pānīyāni pivissantī’ti;
root_text.an3.70:2.8: evamevaṁ kho, visākhe, idhekacco uposathiko iti paṭisañcikkhati:
root_text.an3.70:2.9: ‘ahaṁ khvajja idañcidañca khādanīyaṁ khādiṁ, idañcidañca bhojanīyaṁ bhuñjiṁ;
root_text.an3.70:2.10: sve dānāhaṁ idañcidañca khādanīyaṁ khādissāmi, idañcidañca bhojanīyaṁ bhuñjissāmī’ti.
root_text.an3.70:2.11: So tena abhijjhāsahagatena cetasā divasaṁ atināmeti.
root_text.an3.70:2.13: Evaṁ upavuttho kho, visākhe, gopālakuposatho na mahapphalo hoti na mahānisaṁso na mahājutiko na mahāvipphāro.
root_text.an3.70:3.4: ‘ehi tvaṁ, ambho purisa, ye puratthimāya disāya pāṇā paraṁ yojanasataṁ tesu daṇḍaṁ nikkhipāhi;
root_text.an3.70:3.5: ye pacchimāya disāya pāṇā paraṁ yojanasataṁ tesu daṇḍaṁ nikkhipāhi;
root_text.an3.70:3.6: ye uttarāya disāya pāṇā paraṁ yojanasataṁ tesu daṇḍaṁ nikkhipāhi;
root_text.an3.70:3.7: ye dakkhiṇāya disāya pāṇā paraṁ yojanasataṁ tesu daṇḍaṁ nikkhipāhī’ti.
root_text.an3.70:3.8: Iti ekaccānaṁ pāṇānaṁ anuddayāya anukampāya samādapenti, ekaccānaṁ pāṇānaṁ nānuddayāya nānukampāya samādapenti.
root_text.an3.70:3.10: ‘ehi tvaṁ, ambho purisa, sabbacelāni nikkhipitvā evaṁ vadehi—
root_text.an3.70:3.11: nāhaṁ kvacani kassaci kiñcanatasmiṁ, na ca mama kvacani katthaci kiñcanatatthī’ti.
root_text.an3.70:3.24: Iti yasmiṁ samaye sacce samādapetabbā musāvāde tasmiṁ samaye samādapenti.
root_text.an3.70:3.26: So tassā rattiyā accayena bhoge adinnaṁyeva paribhuñjati.
root_text.an3.70:3.29: Evaṁ upavuttho kho, visākhe, nigaṇṭhuposatho na mahapphalo hoti na mahānisaṁso na mahājutiko na mahāvipphāro.
root_text.an3.70:4.2: Upakkiliṭṭhassa, visākhe, cittassa upakkamena pariyodapanā hoti.
root_text.an3.70:4.3: Kathañca, visākhe, upakkiliṭṭhassa cittassa upakkamena pariyodapanā hoti?
root_text.an3.70:4.5: ‘itipi so bhagavā arahaṁ sammāsambuddho vijjācaraṇasampanno sugato lokavidū anuttaro purisadammasārathi satthā devamanussānaṁ buddho bhagavā’ti.
root_text.an3.70:4.6: Tassa tathāgataṁ anussarato cittaṁ pasīdati, pāmojjaṁ uppajjati, ye cittassa upakkilesā te pahīyanti, seyyathāpi, visākhe, upakkiliṭṭhassa sīsassa upakkamena pariyodapanā hoti.
root_text.an3.70:5.1: Kathañca, visākhe, upakkiliṭṭhassa sīsassa upakkamena pariyodapanā hoti?
root_text.an3.70:5.2: Kakkañca paṭicca mattikañca paṭicca udakañca paṭicca purisassa ca tajjaṁ vāyāmaṁ paṭicca, evaṁ kho, visākhe, upakkiliṭṭhassa sīsassa upakkamena pariyodapanā hoti.
root_text.an3.70:5.3: Evamevaṁ kho, visākhe, upakkiliṭṭhassa cittassa upakkamena pariyodapanā hoti.
root_text.an3.70:6.1: Kathañca, visākhe, upakkiliṭṭhassa cittassa upakkamena pariyodapanā hoti?
root_text.an3.70:6.3: ‘itipi so bhagavā arahaṁ sammāsambuddho vijjācaraṇasampanno sugato lokavidū anuttaro purisadammasārathi satthā devamanussānaṁ buddho bhagavā’ti.
root_text.an3.70:6.4: Tassa tathāgataṁ anussarato cittaṁ pasīdati, pāmojjaṁ uppajjati, ye cittassa upakkilesā te pahīyanti.
root_text.an3.70:6.6: ‘ariyasāvako brahmuposathaṁ upavasati, brahmunā saddhiṁ saṁvasati, brahmañcassa ārabbha cittaṁ pasīdati, pāmojjaṁ uppajjati, ye cittassa upakkilesā te pahīyanti’.
root_text.an3.70:6.7: Evaṁ kho, visākhe, upakkiliṭṭhassa cittassa upakkamena pariyodapanā hoti. .1
root_text.an3.70:7.1: Upakkiliṭṭhassa, visākhe, cittassa upakkamena pariyodapanā hoti.
root_text.an3.70:7.2: Kathañca, visākhe, upakkiliṭṭhassa cittassa upakkamena pariyodapanā hoti?
root_text.an3.70:7.4: ‘svākkhāto bhagavatā dhammo sandiṭṭhiko akāliko ehipassiko opaneyyiko paccattaṁ veditabbo viññūhī’ti.
root_text.an3.70:7.5: Tassa dhammaṁ anussarato cittaṁ pasīdati, pāmojjaṁ uppajjati, ye cittassa upakkilesā te pahīyanti, seyyathāpi, visākhe, upakkiliṭṭhassa kāyassa upakkamena pariyodapanā hoti.
root_text.an3.70:8.1: Kathañca, visākhe, upakkiliṭṭhassa kāyassa upakkamena pariyodapanā hoti?
root_text.an3.70:8.2: Sottiñca paṭicca, cuṇṇañca paṭicca, udakañca paṭicca, purisassa ca tajjaṁ vāyāmaṁ paṭicca.
root_text.an3.70:8.3: Evaṁ kho, visākhe, upakkiliṭṭhassa kāyassa upakkamena pariyodapanā hoti.
root_text.an3.70:8.4: Evamevaṁ kho, visākhe, upakkiliṭṭhassa cittassa upakkamena pariyodapanā hoti.
root_text.an3.70:9.1: Kathañca, visākhe, upakkiliṭṭhassa cittassa upakkamena pariyodapanā hoti?
root_text.an3.70:9.3: ‘svākkhāto bhagavatā dhammo sandiṭṭhiko akāliko ehipassiko opaneyyiko paccattaṁ veditabbo viññūhī’ti.
root_text.an3.70:9.4: Tassa dhammaṁ anussarato cittaṁ pasīdati, pāmojjaṁ uppajjati, ye cittassa upakkilesā te pahīyanti.
root_text.an3.70:9.5: Ayaṁ vuccati, visākhe, ‘ariyasāvako dhammuposathaṁ upavasati, dhammena saddhiṁ saṁvasati, dhammañcassa ārabbha cittaṁ pasīdati, pāmojjaṁ uppajjati, ye cittassa upakkilesā te pahīyanti’.
root_text.an3.70:9.6: Evaṁ kho, visākhe, upakkiliṭṭhassa cittassa upakkamena pariyodapanā hoti. .2
root_text.an3.70:10.1: Upakkiliṭṭhassa, visākhe, cittassa upakkamena pariyodapanā hoti.
root_text.an3.70:10.2: Kathañca, visākhe, upakkiliṭṭhassa cittassa upakkamena pariyodapanā hoti?
root_text.an3.70:10.4: ‘suppaṭipanno bhagavato sāvakasaṅgho, ujuppaṭipanno bhagavato sāvakasaṅgho, ñāyappaṭipanno bhagavato sāvakasaṅgho, sāmīcippaṭipanno bhagavato sāvakasaṅgho, yadidaṁ cattāri purisayugāni aṭṭha purisapuggalā esa bhagavato sāvakasaṅgho āhuneyyo pāhuneyyo dakkhiṇeyyo añjalikaraṇīyo anuttaraṁ puññakkhettaṁ lokassā’ti.
root_text.an3.70:10.5: Tassa saṅghaṁ anussarato cittaṁ pasīdati, pāmojjaṁ uppajjati, ye cittassa upakkilesā te pahīyanti, seyyathāpi, visākhe, upakkiliṭṭhassa vatthassa upakkamena pariyodapanā hoti.
root_text.an3.70:11.1: Kathañca, visākhe, upakkiliṭṭhassa vatthassa upakkamena pariyodapanā hoti?
root_text.an3.70:11.2: Usmañca paṭicca, khārañca paṭicca, gomayañca paṭicca, udakañca paṭicca, purisassa ca tajjaṁ vāyāmaṁ paṭicca.
root_text.an3.70:11.3: Evaṁ kho, visākhe, upakkiliṭṭhassa vatthassa upakkamena pariyodapanā hoti.
root_text.an3.70:11.4: Evamevaṁ kho, visākhe, upakkiliṭṭhassa cittassa upakkamena pariyodapanā hoti.
root_text.an3.70:12.1: Kathañca, visākhe, upakkiliṭṭhassa cittassa upakkamena pariyodapanā hoti?
root_text.an3.70:12.4: Tassa saṅghaṁ anussarato cittaṁ pasīdati, pāmojjaṁ uppajjati, ye cittassa upakkilesā te pahīyanti.
root_text.an3.70:12.5: Ayaṁ vuccati, visākhe, ‘ariyasāvako saṅghuposathaṁ upavasati, saṅghena saddhiṁ saṁvasati, saṅghañcassa ārabbha cittaṁ pasīdati, pāmojjaṁ uppajjati, ye cittassa upakkilesā te pahīyanti’.
root_text.an3.70:12.6: Evaṁ kho, visākhe, upakkiliṭṭhassa cittassa upakkamena pariyodapanā hoti. .3
root_text.an3.70:13.1: Upakkiliṭṭhassa, visākhe, cittassa upakkamena pariyodapanā hoti.
root_text.an3.70:13.2: Kathañca, visākhe, upakkiliṭṭhassa cittassa upakkamena pariyodapanā hoti?
root_text.an3.70:13.3: Idha, visākhe, ariyasāvako attano sīlāni anussarati akhaṇḍāni acchiddāni asabalāni akammāsāni bhujissāni viññuppasatthāni aparāmaṭṭhāni samādhisaṁvattanikāni.
root_text.an3.70:13.4: Tassa sīlaṁ anussarato cittaṁ pasīdati, pāmojjaṁ uppajjati, ye cittassa upakkilesā te pahīyanti, seyyathāpi, visākhe, upakkiliṭṭhassa ādāsassa upakkamena pariyodapanā hoti.
root_text.an3.70:14.1: Kathañca, visākhe, upakkiliṭṭhassa ādāsassa upakkamena pariyodapanā hoti?
root_text.an3.70:14.2: Telañca paṭicca, chārikañca paṭicca, vālaṇḍupakañca paṭicca, purisassa ca tajjaṁ vāyāmaṁ paṭicca.
root_text.an3.70:14.3: Evaṁ kho, visākhe, upakkiliṭṭhassa ādāsassa upakkamena pariyodapanā hoti.
root_text.an3.70:14.4: Evamevaṁ kho, visākhe, upakkiliṭṭhassa cittassa upakkamena pariyodapanā hoti.
root_text.an3.70:15.1: Kathañca, visākhe, upakkiliṭṭhassa cittassa upakkamena pariyodapanā hoti?
root_text.an3.70:15.2: Idha, visākhe, ariyasāvako attano sīlāni anussarati akhaṇḍāni …pe… samādhisaṁvattanikāni.
root_text.an3.70:15.3: Tassa sīlaṁ anussarato cittaṁ pasīdati, pāmojjaṁ uppajjati, ye cittassa upakkilesā te pahīyanti.
root_text.an3.70:15.4: Ayaṁ vuccati, visākhe, ‘ariyasāvako sīluposathaṁ upavasati, sīlena saddhiṁ saṁvasati, sīlañcassa ārabbha cittaṁ pasīdati, pāmojjaṁ uppajjati, ye cittassa upakkilesā te pahīyanti’.
root_text.an3.70:15.5: Evaṁ kho, visākhe, upakkiliṭṭhassa cittassa upakkamena pariyodapanā hoti. .4
root_text.an3.70:16.1: Upakkiliṭṭhassa, visākhe, cittassa upakkamena pariyodapanā hoti.
root_text.an3.70:16.2: Kathañca, visākhe, upakkiliṭṭhassa cittassa upakkamena pariyodapanā hoti?
root_text.an3.70:16.4: ‘santi devā cātumahārājikā, santi devā tāvatiṁsā, santi devā yāmā, santi devā tusitā, santi devā nimmānaratino, santi devā paranimmitavasavattino, santi devā brahmakāyikā, santi devā tatuttari.
root_text.an3.70:16.5: Yathārūpāya saddhāya samannāgatā tā devatā ito cutā tatthupapannā, mayhampi tathārūpā saddhā saṁvijjati.
root_text.an3.70:16.6: Yathārūpena sīlena samannāgatā tā devatā ito cutā tatthupapannā, mayhampi tathārūpaṁ sīlaṁ saṁvijjati.
root_text.an3.70:16.7: Yathārūpena sutena samannāgatā tā devatā ito cutā tatthupapannā, mayhampi tathārūpaṁ sutaṁ saṁvijjati.
root_text.an3.70:16.8: Yathārūpena cāgena samannāgatā tā devatā ito cutā tatthupapannā, mayhampi tathārūpo cāgo saṁvijjati.
root_text.an3.70:16.9: Yathārūpāya paññāya samannāgatā tā devatā ito cutā tatthupapannā, mayhampi tathārūpā paññā saṁvijjatī’ti.
root_text.an3.70:16.10: Tassa attano ca tāsañca devatānaṁ saddhañca sīlañca sutañca cāgañca paññañca anussarato cittaṁ pasīdati, pāmojjaṁ uppajjati, ye cittassa upakkilesā te pahīyanti, seyyathāpi, visākhe, upakkiliṭṭhassa jātarūpassa upakkamena pariyodapanā hoti.
root_text.an3.70:17.1: Kathañca, visākhe, upakkiliṭṭhassa jātarūpassa upakkamena pariyodapanā hoti?
root_text.an3.70:17.2: Ukkañca paṭicca, loṇañca paṭicca, gerukañca paṭicca, nāḷikasaṇḍāsañca paṭicca, purisassa ca tajjaṁ vāyāmaṁ paṭicca.
root_text.an3.70:17.3: Evaṁ kho, visākhe, upakkiliṭṭhassa jātarūpassa upakkamena pariyodapanā hoti.
root_text.an3.70:17.4: Evamevaṁ kho, visākhe, upakkiliṭṭhassa cittassa upakkamena pariyodapanā hoti.
root_text.an3.70:18.1: Kathañca, visākhe, upakkiliṭṭhassa cittassa upakkamena pariyodapanā hoti?
root_text.an3.70:18.3: ‘santi devā cātumahārājikā, santi devā tāvatiṁsā …pe… santi devā tatuttari.
root_text.an3.70:18.4: Yathārūpāya saddhāya samannāgatā tā devatā ito cutā tatthupapannā, mayhampi tathārūpā saddhā saṁvijjati.
root_text.an3.70:18.8: paññāya samannāgatā tā devatā ito cutā tatthupapannā, mayhampi tathārūpā paññā saṁvijjatī’ti.
root_text.an3.70:18.9: Tassa attano ca tāsañca devatānaṁ saddhañca sīlañca sutañca cāgañca paññañca anussarato cittaṁ pasīdati, pāmojjaṁ uppajjati, ye cittassa upakkilesā te pahīyanti.
root_text.an3.70:18.10: Ayaṁ vuccati, visākhe, ‘ariyasāvako devatuposathaṁ upavasati, devatāhi saddhiṁ saṁvasati, devatā ārabbha cittaṁ pasīdati, pāmojjaṁ uppajjati, ye cittassa upakkilesā te pahīyanti’.
root_text.an3.70:18.11: Evaṁ kho, visākhe, upakkiliṭṭhassa cittassa upakkamena pariyodapanā hoti. .5
root_text.an3.70:19.1: Sa kho so, visākhe, ariyasāvako iti paṭisañcikkhati:
root_text.an3.70:19.2: ‘yāvajīvaṁ arahanto pāṇātipātaṁ pahāya pāṇātipātā paṭiviratā nihitadaṇḍā nihitasatthā lajjī dayāpannā sabbapāṇabhūtahitānukampī viharanti;
root_text.an3.70:19.3: ahampajja imañca rattiṁ imañca divasaṁ pāṇātipātaṁ pahāya pāṇātipātā paṭivirato nihitadaṇḍo nihitasattho lajjī dayāpanno sabbapāṇabhūtahitānukampī viharāmi.
root_text.an3.70:19.4: Imināpi aṅgena arahataṁ anukaromi, uposatho ca me upavuttho bhavissati.
root_text.an3.70:20.1: Yāvajīvaṁ arahanto adinnādānaṁ pahāya adinnādānā paṭiviratā dinnādāyī dinnapāṭikaṅkhī, athenena sucibhūtena attanā viharanti;
root_text.an3.70:20.2: ahampajja imañca rattiṁ imañca divasaṁ adinnādānaṁ pahāya adinnādānā paṭivirato dinnādāyī dinnapāṭikaṅkhī, athenena sucibhūtena attanā viharāmi.
root_text.an3.70:20.3: Imināpi aṅgena arahataṁ anukaromi, uposatho ca me upavuttho bhavissati.
root_text.an3.70:21.1: Yāvajīvaṁ arahanto abrahmacariyaṁ pahāya brahmacārī ārācārī viratā methunā gāmadhammā;
root_text.an3.70:21.2: ahampajja imañca rattiṁ imañca divasaṁ abrahmacariyaṁ pahāya brahmacārī ārācārī virato methunā gāmadhammā.
root_text.an3.70:21.3: Imināpi aṅgena arahataṁ anukaromi, uposatho ca me upavuttho bhavissati.
root_text.an3.70:22.1: Yāvajīvaṁ arahanto musāvādaṁ pahāya musāvādā paṭiviratā saccavādī saccasandhā thetā paccayikā avisaṁvādakā lokassa;
root_text.an3.70:22.2: ahampajja imañca rattiṁ imañca divasaṁ musāvādaṁ pahāya musāvādā paṭivirato saccavādī saccasandho theto paccayiko avisaṁvādako lokassa.
root_text.an3.70:22.3: Imināpi aṅgena arahataṁ anukaromi, uposatho ca me upavuttho bhavissati.
root_text.an3.70:23.1: Yāvajīvaṁ arahanto surāmerayamajjapamādaṭṭhānaṁ pahāya surāmerayamajjapamādaṭṭhānā paṭiviratā;
root_text.an3.70:23.2: ahampajja imañca rattiṁ imañca divasaṁ surāmerayamajjapamādaṭṭhānaṁ pahāya surāmerayamajjapamādaṭṭhānā paṭivirato.
root_text.an3.70:23.3: Imināpi aṅgena arahataṁ anukaromi, uposatho ca me upavuttho bhavissati.
root_text.an3.70:24.1: Yāvajīvaṁ arahanto ekabhattikā rattūparatā viratā vikālabhojanā;
root_text.an3.70:24.2: ahampajja imañca rattiṁ imañca divasaṁ ekabhattiko rattūparato virato vikālabhojanā.
root_text.an3.70:24.3: Imināpi aṅgena arahataṁ anukaromi, uposatho ca me upavuttho bhavissati.
root_text.an3.70:25.2: ahampajja imañca rattiṁ imañca divasaṁ naccagītavāditavisūkadassanamālāgandhavilepanadhāraṇamaṇḍanavibhūsanaṭṭhānā paṭivirato.
root_text.an3.70:25.3: Imināpi aṅgena arahataṁ anukaromi, uposatho ca me upavuttho bhavissati.
root_text.an3.70:26.1: Yāvajīvaṁ arahanto uccāsayanamahāsayanaṁ pahāya uccāsayanamahāsayanā paṭiviratā nīcaseyyaṁ kappenti mañcake vā tiṇasanthārake vā;
root_text.an3.70:26.2: ahampajja imañca rattiṁ imañca divasaṁ uccāsayanamahāsayanaṁ pahāya uccāsayanamahāsayanā paṭivirato nīcaseyyaṁ kappemi mañcake vā tiṇasanthārake vā.
root_text.an3.70:26.3: Imināpi aṅgena arahataṁ anukaromi, uposatho ca me upavuttho bhavissatī’ti.
root_text.an3.70:27.2: Evaṁ upavuttho kho, visākhe, ariyuposatho mahapphalo hoti mahānisaṁso mahājutiko mahāvipphāro.
root_text.an3.70:28.2: Seyyathāpi, visākhe, yo imesaṁ soḷasannaṁ mahājanapadānaṁ pahūtarattaratanānaṁ issariyādhipaccaṁ rajjaṁ kāreyya, seyyathidaṁ—
root_text.an3.70:28.3: aṅgānaṁ, magadhānaṁ, kāsīnaṁ, kosalānaṁ, vajjīnaṁ, mallānaṁ, cetīnaṁ, vaṅgānaṁ, kurūnaṁ, pañcālānaṁ, macchānaṁ, sūrasenānaṁ, assakānaṁ, avantīnaṁ, gandhārānaṁ, kambojānaṁ, aṭṭhaṅgasamannāgatassa uposathassa etaṁ kalaṁ nāgghati soḷasiṁ.
root_text.an3.70:28.5: Kapaṇaṁ, visākhe, mānusakaṁ rajjaṁ dibbaṁ sukhaṁ upanidhāya.
root_text.an3.70:29.1: Yāni, visākhe, mānusakāni paññāsa vassāni, cātumahārājikānaṁ devānaṁ eso eko rattindivo.
root_text.an3.70:29.4: Tena saṁvaccharena dibbāni pañca vassasatāni cātumahārājikānaṁ devānaṁ āyuppamāṇaṁ.
root_text.an3.70:29.5: Ṭhānaṁ kho panetaṁ, visākhe, vijjati yaṁ idhekacco itthī vā puriso vā aṭṭhaṅgasamannāgataṁ uposathaṁ upavasitvā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā cātumahārājikānaṁ devānaṁ sahabyataṁ upapajjeyya.
root_text.an3.70:29.6: Idaṁ kho panetaṁ, visākhe, sandhāya bhāsitaṁ:
root_text.an3.70:30.1: Yaṁ, visākhe, mānusakaṁ vassasataṁ, tāvatiṁsānaṁ devānaṁ eso eko rattindivo.
root_text.an3.70:30.4: Tena saṁvaccharena dibbaṁ vassasahassaṁ tāvatiṁsānaṁ devānaṁ āyuppamāṇaṁ.
root_text.an3.70:30.5: Ṭhānaṁ kho panetaṁ, visākhe, vijjati yaṁ idhekacco itthī vā puriso vā aṭṭhaṅgasamannāgataṁ uposathaṁ upavasitvā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā tāvatiṁsānaṁ devānaṁ sahabyataṁ upapajjeyya.
root_text.an3.70:30.6: Idaṁ kho panetaṁ, visākhe, sandhāya bhāsitaṁ:
root_text.an3.70:31.1: Yāni, visākhe, mānusakāni dve vassasatāni, yāmānaṁ devānaṁ eso eko rattindivo.
root_text.an3.70:31.4: Tena saṁvaccharena dibbāni dve vassasahassāni yāmānaṁ devānaṁ āyuppamāṇaṁ.
root_text.an3.70:31.5: Ṭhānaṁ kho panetaṁ, visākhe, vijjati yaṁ idhekacco itthī vā puriso vā aṭṭhaṅgasamannāgataṁ uposathaṁ upavasitvā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā yāmānaṁ devānaṁ sahabyataṁ upapajjeyya.
root_text.an3.70:31.6: Idaṁ kho panetaṁ, visākhe, sandhāya bhāsitaṁ:
root_text.an3.70:32.1: Yāni, visākhe, mānusakāni cattāri vassasatāni, tusitānaṁ devānaṁ eso eko rattindivo.
root_text.an3.70:32.4: Tena saṁvaccharena dibbāni cattāri vassasahassāni tusitānaṁ devānaṁ āyuppamāṇaṁ.
root_text.an3.70:32.5: Ṭhānaṁ kho panetaṁ, visākhe, vijjati yaṁ idhekacco itthī vā puriso vā aṭṭhaṅgasamannāgataṁ uposathaṁ upavasitvā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā tusitānaṁ devānaṁ sahabyataṁ upapajjeyya.
root_text.an3.70:32.6: Idaṁ kho panetaṁ, visākhe, sandhāya bhāsitaṁ:
root_text.an3.70:33.1: Yāni, visākhe, mānusakāni aṭṭha vassasatāni, nimmānaratīnaṁ devānaṁ eso eko rattindivo.
root_text.an3.70:33.4: Tena saṁvaccharena dibbāni aṭṭha vassasahassāni nimmānaratīnaṁ devānaṁ āyuppamāṇaṁ.
root_text.an3.70:33.5: Ṭhānaṁ kho panetaṁ, visākhe, vijjati yaṁ idhekacco itthī vā puriso vā aṭṭhaṅgasamannāgataṁ uposathaṁ upavasitvā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā nimmānaratīnaṁ devānaṁ sahabyataṁ upapajjeyya.
root_text.an3.70:33.6: Idaṁ kho panetaṁ, visākhe, sandhāya bhāsitaṁ:
root_text.an3.70:34.1: Yāni, visākhe, mānusakāni soḷasa vassasatāni, paranimmitavasavattīnaṁ devānaṁ eso eko rattindivo.
root_text.an3.70:34.4: Tena saṁvaccharena dibbāni soḷasa vassasahassāni paranimmitavasavattīnaṁ devānaṁ āyuppamāṇaṁ.
root_text.an3.70:34.5: Ṭhānaṁ kho panetaṁ, visākhe, vijjati yaṁ idhekacco itthī vā puriso vā aṭṭhaṅgasamannāgataṁ uposathaṁ upavasitvā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā paranimmitavasavattīnaṁ devānaṁ sahabyataṁ upapajjeyya.
root_text.an3.70:34.6: Idaṁ kho panetaṁ, visākhe, sandhāya bhāsitaṁ:
root_text.an3.70:35.2: Musā na bhāse na ca majjapo siyā;
root_text.an3.70:36.1: Mālaṁ na dhāre na ca gandhamācare,
root_text.an3.70:37.1: Cando ca suriyo ca ubho sudassanā,
root_text.an3.70:40.1: Tasmā hi nārī ca naro ca sīlavā,

translation_text.an3.70:1.2: At one time the Buddha was staying near Sāvatthī in the stilt longhouse of Migāra’s mother in the Eastern Monastery.
translation_text.an3.70:1.3: Then Visākhā, Migāra’s mother, went up to the Buddha, bowed, and sat down to one side. The Buddha said to her,
translation_text.an3.70:1.4: “So, Visākhā, where are you coming from in the middle of the day?”
translation_text.an3.70:2.3: The sabbath of the cowherds, the sabbath of the Jains, and the sabbath of the noble ones.
translation_text.an3.70:2.4: And what is the sabbath of the cowherds?
translation_text.an3.70:2.5: It’s just like a cowherd who, in the late afternoon, takes the cows back to their owners. They reflect:
translation_text.an3.70:2.6: ‘Today the cows grazed in this spot and that, and they drank in this spot and that.
translation_text.an3.70:2.7: Tomorrow the cows will graze in this spot and that, and drink in this spot and that.’
translation_text.an3.70:2.8: In the same way, someone keeping the sabbath reflects:
translation_text.an3.70:2.9: ‘Today I ate this and that, and had a meal of this and that.
translation_text.an3.70:2.10: Tomorrow I’ll eat this and that, and have a meal of this and that.’
translation_text.an3.70:2.11: And so they spend their day with a mind full of covetousness.
translation_text.an3.70:2.12: That’s the sabbath of the cowherds.
translation_text.an3.70:2.13: When the cowherd’s sabbath is observed like this it’s not very fruitful or beneficial or dazzling or bountiful.
translation_text.an3.70:3.1: And what is the sabbath of the Jains?
translation_text.an3.70:3.2: There’s a kind of ascetic belonging to a group called the Jains.
translation_text.an3.70:3.4: ‘Please, good people, don’t hurt any living creatures more than a hundred leagues away to the east.
translation_text.an3.70:3.5: Don’t hurt any living creatures more than a hundred leagues away to the west.
translation_text.an3.70:3.6: Don’t hurt any living creatures more than a hundred leagues away to the north.
translation_text.an3.70:3.7: Don’t hurt any living creatures more than a hundred leagues away to the south.’
translation_text.an3.70:3.8: So they encourage kindness and sympathy for some creatures and not others.
translation_text.an3.70:3.9: On the sabbath, they encourage their disciples:
translation_text.an3.70:3.10: ‘Please, good people, take off all your clothes and say:
translation_text.an3.70:3.11: “I don’t belong to anyone anywhere! And nothing belongs to me anywhere!”’
translation_text.an3.70:3.12: But their mother and father still know,
translation_text.an3.70:3.20: Bondservants, workers, and staff still know:
translation_text.an3.70:3.23: ‘These are my bondservants, workers, and staff.’
translation_text.an3.70:3.24: So, at a time when they should be encouraged to speak the truth, the Jains encourage them to lie.
translation_text.an3.70:3.26: When the night has passed they use their possessions once more, though they’ve not been given back to them.
translation_text.an3.70:3.28: That’s the sabbath of the Jains.
translation_text.an3.70:3.29: When the Jain’s sabbath is observed like this it’s not very fruitful or beneficial or dazzling or bountiful.
translation_text.an3.70:4.1: And what is the sabbath of the noble ones?
translation_text.an3.70:4.2: A corrupt mind is cleaned by applying effort.
translation_text.an3.70:4.3: And how is a corrupt mind cleaned by applying effort?
translation_text.an3.70:4.4: It’s when a noble disciple recollects the Realized One:
translation_text.an3.70:4.5: ‘That Blessed One is perfected, a fully awakened Buddha, accomplished in knowledge and conduct, holy, knower of the world, supreme guide for those fit for training, teacher of gods and humans, awakened, blessed.’
translation_text.an3.70:4.6: As they recollect the Realized One, their mind becomes clear, joy arises, and mental corruptions are given up. It’s just like cleaning a dirty head by applying effort.
translation_text.an3.70:5.1: And how is a dirty head cleaned by applying effort?
translation_text.an3.70:5.2: With cleansing paste, clay, and water, and by applying the appropriate effort.
translation_text.an3.70:5.3: In the same way, a corrupt mind is cleaned by applying effort.
translation_text.an3.70:6.1: And how is a corrupt mind cleaned by applying effort?
translation_text.an3.70:6.2: It’s when a noble disciple recollects the Realized One:
translation_text.an3.70:6.3: ‘That Blessed One is perfected, a fully awakened Buddha, accomplished in knowledge and conduct, holy, knower of the world, supreme guide for those fit for training, teacher of gods and humans, awakened, blessed.’
translation_text.an3.70:6.4: As they recollect the Realized One, their mind becomes clear, joy arises, and mental corruptions are given up.
translation_text.an3.70:6.6: ‘A noble disciple who observes the sabbath of the divinity, living together with the divinity. And because they think of the divinity their mind becomes clear, joy arises, and mental corruptions are given up.’
translation_text.an3.70:6.7: That’s how a corrupt mind is cleaned by applying effort.
translation_text.an3.70:7.1: A corrupt mind is cleaned by applying effort.
translation_text.an3.70:7.2: And how is a corrupt mind cleaned by applying effort?
translation_text.an3.70:7.3: It’s when a noble disciple recollects the teaching:
translation_text.an3.70:7.4: ‘The teaching is well explained by the Buddha—apparent in the present life, immediately effective, inviting inspection, relevant, so that sensible people can know it for themselves.’
translation_text.an3.70:7.5: As they recollect the teaching, their mind becomes clear, joy arises, and mental corruptions are given up. It’s just like cleaning a dirty body by applying effort.
translation_text.an3.70:8.1: And how is a dirty body cleaned by applying effort?
translation_text.an3.70:8.2: With cleanser and powder, water, and by applying the appropriate effort.
translation_text.an3.70:8.3: That’s how a dirty body is cleaned by applying effort.
translation_text.an3.70:8.4: In the same way, a corrupt mind is cleaned by applying effort.
translation_text.an3.70:9.1: And how is a corrupt mind cleaned by applying effort?
translation_text.an3.70:9.2: It’s when a noble disciple recollects the teaching:
translation_text.an3.70:9.3: ‘The teaching is well explained by the Buddha—apparent in the present life, immediately effective, inviting inspection, relevant, so that sensible people can know it for themselves.’
translation_text.an3.70:9.4: As they recollect the teaching, their mind becomes clear, joy arises, and mental corruptions are given up.
translation_text.an3.70:9.5: This is called: ‘A noble disciple who observes the sabbath of Dhamma, living together with Dhamma. And because they think of the Dhamma their mind becomes clear, joy arises, and mental corruptions are given up.’
translation_text.an3.70:9.6: That’s how a corrupt mind is cleaned by applying effort.
translation_text.an3.70:10.1: A corrupt mind is cleaned by applying effort.
translation_text.an3.70:10.2: And how is a corrupt mind cleaned by applying effort?
translation_text.an3.70:10.3: It’s when a noble disciple recollects the Saṅgha:
translation_text.an3.70:10.4: ‘The Saṅgha of the Buddha’s disciples is practicing the way that’s good, direct, systematic, and proper. It consists of the four pairs, the eight individual persons. This is the Saṅgha of the Buddha’s disciples that is worthy of offerings dedicated to the gods, worthy of hospitality, worthy of a religious donation, worthy of greeting with joined palms, and is the supreme field of merit for the world.’
translation_text.an3.70:10.5: As they recollect the Saṅgha, their mind becomes clear, joy arises, and mental corruptions are given up. It’s just like cleaning a dirty cloth by applying effort.
translation_text.an3.70:11.1: And how is a dirty cloth cleaned by applying effort?
translation_text.an3.70:11.2: With salt, lye, cow dung, and water, and by applying the appropriate effort.
translation_text.an3.70:11.3: That’s how a dirty cloth is cleaned by applying effort.
translation_text.an3.70:11.4: In the same way, a corrupt mind is cleaned by applying effort.
translation_text.an3.70:12.1: And how is a corrupt mind cleaned by applying effort?
translation_text.an3.70:12.2: It’s when a noble disciple recollects the Saṅgha:
translation_text.an3.70:12.3: ‘The Saṅgha of the Buddha’s disciples is practicing the way that’s good, direct, systematic, and proper. It consists of the four pairs, the eight individual persons. This Saṅgha of the Buddha’s disciples is worthy of offerings dedicated to the gods, worthy of hospitality, worthy of a religious donation, and worthy of veneration with joined palms. It is the supreme field of merit for the world.’
translation_text.an3.70:12.4: As they recollect the Saṅgha, their mind becomes clear, joy arises, and mental corruptions are given up.
translation_text.an3.70:12.5: This is called: ‘A noble disciple who observes the sabbath of the Saṅgha, living together with the Saṅgha. And because they think of the Saṅgha their mind becomes clear, joy arises, and mental corruptions are given up.’
translation_text.an3.70:12.6: That’s how a corrupt mind is cleaned by applying effort.
translation_text.an3.70:13.1: A corrupt mind is cleaned by applying effort.
translation_text.an3.70:13.2: And how is a corrupt mind cleaned by applying effort?
translation_text.an3.70:13.3: It’s when a noble disciple recollects their own ethical conduct, which is intact, impeccable, spotless, and unmarred, liberating, praised by sensible people, not mistaken, and leading to immersion.
translation_text.an3.70:13.4: As they recollect their ethical conduct, their mind becomes clear, joy arises, and mental corruptions are given up. It’s just like cleaning a dirty mirror by applying effort.
translation_text.an3.70:14.1: And how is a dirty mirror cleaned by applying effort?
translation_text.an3.70:14.2: With oil, ash, a rolled-up cloth, and by applying the appropriate effort.
translation_text.an3.70:14.3: That’s how a dirty mirror is cleaned by applying effort.
translation_text.an3.70:14.4: In the same way, a corrupt mind is cleaned by applying effort.
translation_text.an3.70:15.1: And how is a corrupt mind cleaned by applying effort?
translation_text.an3.70:15.2: It’s when a noble disciple recollects their own ethical conduct, which is intact, impeccable, spotless, and unmarred, liberating, praised by sensible people, not mistaken, and leading to immersion.
translation_text.an3.70:15.3: As they recollect their ethical conduct, their mind becomes clear, joy arises, and mental corruptions are given up.
translation_text.an3.70:15.4: This is called: ‘A noble disciple who observes the sabbath of ethical conduct, living together with ethics. And because they think of their ethical conduct their mind becomes clear, joy arises, and mental corruptions are given up.’
translation_text.an3.70:15.5: That’s how a corrupt mind is cleaned by applying effort.
translation_text.an3.70:16.1: A corrupt mind is cleaned by applying effort.
translation_text.an3.70:16.2: And how is a corrupt mind cleaned by applying effort?
translation_text.an3.70:16.3: It’s when a noble disciple recollects the deities:
translation_text.an3.70:16.4: ‘There are the gods of the four great kings, the gods of the thirty-three, the gods of Yama, the joyful gods, the gods who love to create, the gods who control what is created by others, the gods of the Divinity’s host, and gods even higher than these.
translation_text.an3.70:16.5: When those deities passed away from here, they were reborn there because of their faith, ethics, learning, generosity, and wisdom. I, too, have the same kind of faith, ethics, learning, generosity, and wisdom.’
translation_text.an3.70:16.10: As they recollect the faith, ethics, learning, generosity, and wisdom of both themselves and those deities, their mind becomes clear, joy arises, and mental corruptions are given up. It’s just like cleansing corrupt native gold by applying effort.
translation_text.an3.70:17.1: And how is corrupt native gold cleansed by applying effort?
translation_text.an3.70:17.2: With a furnace, flux, a blowpipe, and tongs, and by applying the appropriate effort.
translation_text.an3.70:17.3: That’s how corrupt native gold is cleansed by applying effort.
translation_text.an3.70:17.4: In the same way, a corrupt mind is cleaned by applying effort.
translation_text.an3.70:18.1: And how is a corrupt mind cleaned by applying effort?
translation_text.an3.70:18.2: It’s when a noble disciple recollects the deities:
translation_text.an3.70:18.3: ‘There are the gods of the four great kings, the gods of the thirty-three, the gods of Yama, the joyful gods, the gods who love to imagine, the gods who control what is imagined by others, the gods of the Divinity’s host, and gods even higher than these.
translation_text.an3.70:18.4: When those deities passed away from here, they were reborn there because of their faith, ethics, learning, generosity, and wisdom. I, too, have the same kind of faith, ethics, learning, generosity, and wisdom.’
translation_text.an3.70:18.9: As they recollect the faith, ethics, learning, generosity, and wisdom of both themselves and those deities, their mind becomes clear, joy arises, and mental corruptions are given up.
translation_text.an3.70:18.10: This is called: ‘A noble disciple who observes the sabbath of the deities, living together with the deities. And because they think of the deities their mind becomes clear, joy arises, and mental corruptions are given up.’
translation_text.an3.70:18.11: That’s how a corrupt mind is cleaned by applying effort.
translation_text.an3.70:19.2: ‘As long as they live, the perfected ones give up killing living creatures, renouncing the rod and the sword. They are scrupulous and kind, and live full of sympathy for all living beings.
translation_text.an3.70:19.3: I, too, for this day and night will give up killing living creatures, renouncing the rod and the sword. I’ll be scrupulous and kind, and live full of sympathy for all living beings.
translation_text.an3.70:19.4: I will observe the sabbath by doing as the perfected ones do in this respect.
translation_text.an3.70:20.1: As long as they live, the perfected ones give up stealing. They take only what’s given, and expect only what’s given. They keep themselves clean by not thieving.
translation_text.an3.70:20.2: I, too, for this day and night will give up stealing. I’ll take only what’s given, and expect only what’s given. I’ll keep myself clean by not thieving.
translation_text.an3.70:20.3: I will observe the sabbath by doing as the perfected ones do in this respect.
translation_text.an3.70:21.1: As long as they live, the perfected ones give up unchastity. They are chaste, set apart, avoiding the vulgar act of sex.
translation_text.an3.70:21.2: I, too, for this day and night will give up unchastity. I will be chaste, set apart, avoiding the vulgar act of sex.
translation_text.an3.70:21.3: I will observe the sabbath by doing as the perfected ones do in this respect.
translation_text.an3.70:22.1: As long as they live, the perfected ones give up lying. They speak the truth and stick to the truth. They’re honest and dependable, and don’t trick the world with their words.
translation_text.an3.70:22.2: I, too, for this day and night will give up lying. I’ll speak the truth and stick to the truth. I’ll be honest and dependable, and won’t trick the world with my words.
translation_text.an3.70:22.3: I will observe the sabbath by doing as the perfected ones do in this respect.
translation_text.an3.70:23.1: As long as they live, the perfected ones give up beer, wine, and liquor intoxicants.
translation_text.an3.70:23.2: I, too, for this day and night will give up beer, wine, and liquor intoxicants.
translation_text.an3.70:23.3: I will observe the sabbath by doing as the perfected ones do in this respect.
translation_text.an3.70:24.1: As long as they live, the perfected ones eat in one part of the day, abstaining from eating at night and from food at the wrong time.
translation_text.an3.70:24.2: I, too, for this day and night will eat in one part of the day, abstaining from eating at night and food at the wrong time.
translation_text.an3.70:24.3: I will observe the sabbath by doing as the perfected ones do in this respect.
translation_text.an3.70:25.1: As long as they live, the perfected ones refrain from seeing shows of dancing, singing, and music; and attiring and adorning themselves with garlands, fragrance, and makeup.
translation_text.an3.70:25.2: I, too, for this day and night will refrain from seeing shows of dancing, singing, and music ; and attiring and adorning myself with garlands, fragrance, and makeup.
translation_text.an3.70:25.3: I will observe the sabbath by doing as the perfected ones do in this respect.
translation_text.an3.70:26.1: As long as they live, the perfected ones give up high and luxurious beds. They sleep in a low place, either a cot or a straw mat.
translation_text.an3.70:26.2: I, too, for this day and night will give up high and luxurious beds. I’ll sleep in a low place, either a cot or a straw mat.
translation_text.an3.70:26.3: I will observe the sabbath by doing as the perfected ones do in this respect.’
translation_text.an3.70:27.1: That’s the sabbath of the noble ones.
translation_text.an3.70:27.2: When the sabbath of the noble ones is observed like this it’s very fruitful and beneficial and dazzling and bountiful.
translation_text.an3.70:28.2: Suppose you were to rule as sovereign lord over these sixteen great countries—Aṅga, Magadha, Kāsi, Kosala, Vajji, Malla, Cetī, Vaccha, Kuru, Pañcāla, Maccha, Sūrasena, Assaka, Avanti, Gandhāra, and Kamboja—full of the seven treasures.
translation_text.an3.70:28.3: This wouldn’t be worth a sixteenth part of the sabbath with its eight factors.
translation_text.an3.70:28.5: Because human kingship is a poor thing compared to the happiness of the gods.
translation_text.an3.70:29.1: Fifty years in the human realm is one day and night for the gods of the four great kings.
translation_text.an3.70:29.2: Thirty such days make up a month.
translation_text.an3.70:29.3: Twelve such months make up a year.
translation_text.an3.70:29.4: The lifespan of the gods of the four great kings is five hundred of these heavenly years.
translation_text.an3.70:29.5: It’s possible that some woman or man who has observed the eight-factored sabbath will—when their body breaks up, after death—be reborn in the company of the gods of the four great kings.
translation_text.an3.70:29.6: This is what I was referring to when I said:
translation_text.an3.70:29.7: ‘Human kingship is a poor thing compared to the happiness of the gods.’
translation_text.an3.70:30.1: A hundred years in the human realm is one day and night for the gods of the thirty-three.
translation_text.an3.70:30.2: Thirty such days make up a month.
translation_text.an3.70:30.3: Twelve such months make up a year.
translation_text.an3.70:30.4: The lifespan of the gods of the thirty-three is a thousand of these heavenly years.
translation_text.an3.70:30.5: It’s possible that some woman or man who has observed the eight-factored sabbath will—when their body breaks up, after death—be reborn in the company of the gods of the thirty-three.
translation_text.an3.70:30.6: This is what I was referring to when I said:
translation_text.an3.70:30.7: ‘Human kingship is a poor thing compared to the happiness of the gods.’
translation_text.an3.70:31.1: Two hundred years in the human realm is one day and night for the gods of Yama.
translation_text.an3.70:31.2: Thirty such days make up a month.
translation_text.an3.70:31.3: Twelve such months make up a year.
translation_text.an3.70:31.4: The lifespan of the gods of Yama is two thousand of these heavenly years.
translation_text.an3.70:31.5: It’s possible that some woman or man who has observed the eight-factored sabbath will—when their body breaks up, after death—be reborn in the company of the gods of Yama.
translation_text.an3.70:31.6: This is what I was referring to when I said:
translation_text.an3.70:31.7: ‘Human kingship is a poor thing compared to the happiness of the gods.’
translation_text.an3.70:32.1: Four hundred years in the human realm is one day and night for the joyful gods.
translation_text.an3.70:32.2: Thirty such days make up a month.
translation_text.an3.70:32.3: Twelve such months make up a year.
translation_text.an3.70:32.4: The lifespan of the joyful gods is four thousand of these heavenly years.
translation_text.an3.70:32.5: It’s possible that some woman or man who has observed the eight-factored sabbath will—when their body breaks up, after death—be reborn in the company of the joyful gods.
translation_text.an3.70:32.6: This is what I was referring to when I said:
translation_text.an3.70:32.7: ‘Human kingship is a poor thing compared to the happiness of the gods.’
translation_text.an3.70:33.1: Eight hundred years in the human realm is one day and night for the gods who love to create.
translation_text.an3.70:33.2: Thirty such days make up a month.
translation_text.an3.70:33.3: Twelve such months make up a year.
translation_text.an3.70:33.4: The life span of the gods who love to create is eight thousand of these heavenly years.
translation_text.an3.70:33.5: It’s possible that some woman or man who has observed the eight-factored sabbath will—when their body breaks up, after death—be reborn in the company of the gods who love to create.
translation_text.an3.70:33.6: This is what I was referring to when I said:
translation_text.an3.70:33.7: ‘Human kingship is a poor thing compared to the happiness of the gods.’
translation_text.an3.70:34.1: Sixteen hundred years in the human realm is one day and night for the gods who control what is created by others.
translation_text.an3.70:34.2: Thirty such days make up a month.
translation_text.an3.70:34.3: Twelve such months make up a year.
translation_text.an3.70:34.4: The life span of the gods who control what is created by others is sixteen thousand of these heavenly years.
translation_text.an3.70:34.5: It’s possible that some woman or man who has observed the eight-factored sabbath will—when their body breaks up, after death—be reborn in the company of the gods who control what is created by others.
translation_text.an3.70:34.6: This is what I was referring to when I said:
translation_text.an3.70:34.7: ‘Human kingship is a poor thing compared to the happiness of the gods.’
translation_text.an3.70:35.1: You shouldn’t kill living creatures, or steal,
translation_text.an3.70:35.4: and don’t eat at night, the wrong time.
translation_text.an3.70:36.1: Not wearing garlands or applying fragrance,
translation_text.an3.70:36.2: you should sleep on a low bed, <j>or a mat on the ground.
translation_text.an3.70:36.3: This is the eight-factored sabbath, they say,
translation_text.an3.70:36.4: explained by the Buddha, <j>who has gone to suffering’s end.
translation_text.an3.70:37.1: The moon and sun are both fair to see,
translation_text.an3.70:37.2: radiating as far as they revolve.
translation_text.an3.70:37.3: Those dispellers of darkness, travellers in midair,
translation_text.an3.70:37.4: shine in the firmament, lighting up the quarters.
translation_text.an3.70:38.1: All of the wealth that’s found in this realm—
translation_text.an3.70:39.1: they’re not worth a sixteenth part
translation_text.an3.70:39.2: of the sabbath with its eight factors,
translation_text.an3.70:39.3: as starlight cannot rival the moon.
translation_text.an3.70:40.1: So an ethical woman or man,
translation_text.an3.70:40.2: who has observed the eight-factored sabbath,
translation_text.an3.70:40.3: having made merit whose outcome is happiness,
translation_text.an3.70:40.4: blameless, they go to a heavenly place.”

variant_text.an3.70:3.11: kiñcanatasmiṁ → kiñcanattasmiṁ (bj); kiñcanaṁ tasmiṁ (si, sya-all, pts1ed); kiñcanatasmi (?) | katthaci → kismiñci (sya-all); kassaci (pts1ed) | kiñcanatatthī’ti → kiñcana nātthīti (bj); kiñcanatthīti (sya-all); kiñcanaṁ natthīti (pts1ed)
variant_text.an3.70:16.4: cātumahārājikā → cātummahārājikā (bj, sya-all, km, pts1ed) | tatuttari → tatuttariṁ (bj, pts1ed); taduttari (sya-all)
variant_text.an3.70:17.2: gerukañca paṭicca, nāḷikasaṇḍāsañca → nāḷikañca paṭicca saṇḍāsañca (pts1ed, mr)
variant_text.an3.70:28.2: pahūtarattaratanānaṁ → pahūtasattaratanānaṁ (bj, sya-all, km); pahūtamahāsattaratanānaṁ (pts1ed) | issariyādhipaccaṁ → issarādhipaccaṁ (pts1ed)
variant_text.an3.70:35.1: na haññe → na hāne (bj, pts1ed); na hane (mr)
